description_long: This is a classic wooden barrel with a cylindrical shape, slightly bulging at the center and tapering towards the top and bottom. The exterior is constructed from vertical wooden staves, each showing a weathered, textured grain with visible wear and aging. The barrel is reinforced by four dark metal hoops or bands, evenly spaced around its circumference, which provide structural integrity and a rustic appearance. The top and bottom surfaces are flat and made of the same wood as the staves, with the top appearing to be securely closed and flush with the rim. There are no visible handles, spouts, or openings, and the surface finish is matte with a rough, tactile quality. The overall look suggests it is designed for storage or transport of goods, commonly found in urban outdoor or warehouse settings.
description: A weathered wooden barrel with dark metal hoops, bulging sides, and a closed flat top, showing rustic wear and matte finish.
description_view_0: Front view of the barrel showing the full curvature, wood grain, and all four metal hoops clearly.
description_view_1: Slightly rotated view revealing the same features, with subtle changes in wood texture and lighting on the left side.
description_view_2: Another rotated angle, highlighting the right side curvature and consistent banding, with no new features visible.
description_view_3: Back view, nearly identical to the front, showing the same wood and metal details with minor texture variation.
